chunks.text:
Experiences of children encountering online content promoting eating disorders, self-harm and suicide
Experiences of children encountering online content promoting eating disorders, self-harm and suicide
Online safety
Protecting children
Research, statistics and data
Published:
15 March 2024
This research explores the pathways through which children encounter content related to suicide, self-harm and eating disorders, the impact this can have, and perceptions of safety measures. It also provides an overview of children’s description of what content promoting suicide, self-harm and eating disorders looks like online, which children in our sample had high level of familiarity with and characterised it as being prolific on social media.
Content warning:
This report contains distressing discussions of children and young people’s experiences with suicide, self-harm and eating disorder content and behaviours.
